reasons: Valir can push Argus away and slow him, wasting Argus's limited chase and ultimate timing.
This handles Argus without feeding too much damage into Eternal Evil.

proof.summary: Argus wants to stay attached during his death-immunity window, while Valir can deny melee uptime with pushback and slows.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves displacement until Argus commits.
Valir kites backward instead of fighting Argus during Eternal Evil.
failureCases: Valir wastes Searing Torrent before Argus activates Eternal Evil.

reasons: Minsitthar can limit Argus's movement-skill chase inside King's Calling.
This helps teammates disengage instead of fighting Argus during his strongest window.

proof.summary: Argus depends on sticking to targets, while Minsitthar can create a field that restricts movement skills and slows Argus's chase.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts King's Calling after Argus commits.
Teammates disengage instead of fighting Argus during his strongest timing.
failureCases: Argus enters after King's Calling expires.

reasons: Franco can suppress Argus and waste part of his active fighting window.
This is strongest when Franco coordinates with teammates to disengage or punish Argus after Eternal Evil ends.

proof.summary: Argus wants to attack continuously during his survival window, while Franco can deny actions and burn time with suppression.
worksBestWhen: Franco suppresses Argus after Eternal Evil is committed.
Teammates disengage during the suppression window.
failureCases: Argus still has enough time to continue fighting after suppression ends.

reasons: Kaja can drag Argus away from priority targets and force him to spend time repositioning.
Kaja is useful when the team avoids wasting damage into Argus during Eternal Evil.

proof.summary: Argus wants uninterrupted melee uptime, while Kaja can suppress and drag him away from his preferred target path.
worksBestWhen: Kaja drags Argus away from the backline.
Kaja coordinates with teammates to punish after Eternal Evil ends.
failureCases: Argus baits Divine Judgment before committing Eternal Evil.
Kaja pulls Argus into a teammate who cannot survive.

reasons: Baxia reduces shield and HP regeneration, which helps limit Argus's recovery value after forced trades.
Baxia also gives the team a durable body that can absorb Argus's engage better than fragile backliners.

proof.summary: Argus becomes threatening when he survives and recovers after a committed fight, while Baxia can reduce enemy Shield and HP Regen during the trade.
worksBestWhen: Baxia marks Argus before or during his committed melee trade.
Baxia zones Argus away from fragile backline targets.
failureCases: Argus ignores Baxia and reaches the backline.
Baxia cannot keep Argus marked during the important recovery window.
